title: phyllanthus nigericus
intro: Phyllanthus niruri, also known as Chanca Piedra, is a small, herbaceous plant native to tropical regions. It is a member of the Phyllanthaceae family and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating kidney stones and gallstones due to its ability to dissolve calcium oxalate crystals. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits, including reducing blood pressure, improving liver function, and acting as an antiviral agent. Chanca Piedra can be prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is often used in traditional herbal medicine, particularly in Latin America and Asia.
